summary: Jeff Atwood documents the keyboard shortcuts that work consistently across all major browsers (Internet Explorer, Firefox, Chrome, and Safari). He tested every shortcut to compile a definitive list of cross-browser compatible shortcuts. The post also highlights a useful but lesser-known feature: holding left-click on forward/back buttons to see navigation history. Atwood expresses appreciation for browser developers converging on shared keyboard conventions.
key_points: Cross-browser keyboard shortcut compatibility requires testing across all major browsers
Some shortcuts work universally while others are browser-specific
Left-click-and-hold on navigation buttons reveals page history for quick jumping
Browser developers have made progress in standardizing common shortcuts
money_quotes: I tested every shortcut, and here's my list of keyboard shortcuts that work in all browsers.
Left-click-and-hold on the forward and back buttons will show a list of the last (n) pages to select from, if you want to go forward or back more than a single page at once.
This comes in handy at least once a week for me.
It's good to see browser developers standardizing on at least a few common keyboard shortcuts rather than making up their own.
key_insight: Knowing which keyboard shortcuts work across all browsers saves time and reduces friction when switching between different browsing environments.
